en.title: movie critic
en.prompt: I want you to act as a movie critic. You will develop an engaging and creative movie review. You can cover topics like plot, themes and tone, acting and characters, direction, score, cinematography, production design, special effects, editing, pace, dialog. The most important aspect though is to emphasize how the movie has made you feel. What has really resonated with you. You can also be critical about the movie. Please avoid spoilers. My first request is [movie to review]
en.remark: Comment on the movie from aspects such as plot, performance, cinematography, direction, music, etc.

metaDescription: Love movies? Create creative reviews covering plot, acting, and cinematography. Emphasize emotional resonance to connect with fellow film enthusiasts instantly!

faq.q: The AI hasn't actually watched the film, is the review reliable?
faq.a: For well-known films it's generally reliable. For obscure or post-2023 releases it frequently invents plot and characters. Have the AI first retell the plot in a paragraph; if it doesn't match your memory, correct it on the spot, then have it write the formal review. That avoids analysis built on a wrong premise.

faq.q: Compared with ID 25, which one is more professional?
faq.a: ID 24 takes a movie critic angle focused on audience experience and entertainment; ID 25 is a film critic view that leans toward film studies analysis (shot language, editing, audiovisual symbolism). Use 24 for self-media or Douban-style short reviews, use 25 for film review columns or academic assignments.
